name: Visibility utilities
description: Control over whether an element is visually/accessibly rendered (without changing the document layout) and the visually-hidden accessibility pattern.

families.name: visibility
guidance: Use 'collapse' on a table cell, row, or column to hide it without the browser having to rerender the entire table.
You can reveal a child element with 'visibility:visible' even if the parent is 'hidden'

families.name: visually-hidden
families.description: Hides content visually while keeping it available to assistive technology.
guidance: Use for screen-reader-only text (icon button labels, skip links at rest, table caption context).
Never use on focusable elements unless focus reveals them as a focused but invisible element can strand keyboard users.
families.a11y: Core accessibility primitive: content stays in the accessibility tree.
